metadata.title: Topological Quantization of Charge and Spin
metadata.creator: Daniel Alexander Trawin

master_row.title: Topological Quantization of Charge and Spin
master_row.canonical_statement: The integral of the gauge field along the fold yields quantized charge; the spin-half arises from the twist in how the defect is stitched into the hypersphere; these topological features enforce discrete values.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an algebraic definition relating n, S, m.
master_row.dictionary: A fold carrying an electron picks up a discrete (U(1)) gauge charge of (-1) and a phase reversal under (2) rotation, giving spin (1/2); the quantization of charge and spin stems from topological properties of the vacuum field【952647870353647†L255-L269】.
master_row.encyclopedia: Technical interpretation remains tied to the local registry and equation witness. Interpretive role: Charge and spin are discrete because the vacuum fold supports only topological classes. Equation grounding: The local equation anchors are 'latex': 'A fold carrying an electron picks up a discrete U(1)U(1)U(1) gauge charge of -1-1-1 and a phase reversal under 2$$2 pi2$$ rotation, giving spin 1/21/21/2; the quantization of charge and spin stems from topological properties of the vacuum field.', 'mathml': '', 'wolfram': '', 'canonical': 'A fold carrying an electron picks up a discrete U(1)U(1)U(1) gauge charge of -1-1-1 and a phase reversal under 2$$2 pi2$$ rotation, giving spin 1/21/21/2; the quantization of charge and spin stems from topological properties of the vacuum field.'.
